items.title: Rising fuel costs force Australians to rethink Easter holidays

children.content: Rising fuel prices driven by the Middle East conflict are hitting the domestic tourism sector, with cancellations surging ahead of the Easter long weekend.

items.title: Australian fears about Iran war run deeper than an anti-Trump attitude

children.content: The pandemic demonstrated that when global events become local, Australians are quick to turn on even a mighty trading partner. There is a real capacity here for the war to sour our attitude to the US alliance.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

items.title: Decade-old decisions come home to roost, leaving a different oil shock

children.content: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

image.altText: Towers at an oil refinery.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Iran warns US against ground invasion as key diplomats meet in Pakistan for talks

children.content: Iran's parliament speaker says Iran is prepared to confront any American forces on its soil and would set troops "on fire" in its warning against a US ground invasion.

image.altText: A young male marine with dark skin and a black moustache looks into the distance

items.title: At the 'Gate of Tears', a new threat to global energy emerges

children.content: The opening of a new front in the Middle East war has put another vital shipping route on edge. Known in Arabic as the "Gate of Tears", analysts warn this narrow waterway could soon live up to its name.

image.altText: A satellite view of the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

items.title: 'Out of the pocket of taxpayers': Unis spend $1.8b on consultants and contractors

children.content: Consultancies have been accused of infiltrating universities and wasting scarce public funds on questionable advice about cutting courses and jobs.

gallery.altText: A student walks by several others sitting in a grassy area. Behind them is a modern glass building.

thumbnail.altText: A student walks by several others sitting in a grassy area. Behind them is a modern glass building.

items.title: Do you know who owns your GP clinic? The answer might surprise you

children.content: Major insurers like Medibank Private and Bupa are buying up GP clinics in a move that could lead to US-style managed care.

image.altText: A MyHealth medical sign

items.title: Bondi nanny accused of torture for Pinochet makes last stand to evade extradition

children.content: Adriana Rivas has been a fugitive in Australia since fleeing Chile while on bail 20 years ago. A federal court hearing will determine whether she is removed to face trial for the alleged crimes of torture and kidnapping, committed on behalf of the Pinochet regime.

image.altText: Close up of a woman with dark, short hair and glasses.

items.title: Two jobs, no home: The 'almost hidden crisis' hurting single-parent families

children.content: Hunter was plunged into homelessness when his family lost their rental. It is an increasingly similar story to other single parents finding themselves "lifelong renters".

gallery.altText: A young man smiles in front of branches.

thumbnail.altText: A young man smiles in front of branches.

items.title: Liberals dump Moira Deeming from ticket for next state election

children.content: Controversial Victorian MP Moira Deeming has lost her preselection battle for the state election, with Liberal Party members replacing her with business leader Dinesh Gourisetty.

image.altText: Moira Deeming appears serious as she walks through the parliament carpark, wearing a purple blazer.

items.title: Quick hits: F1 driver limps from crash, teenager sets more records

children.content: Oliver Bearman limped away from a big crash after he needed to take evasive action. Here are the quick hits from the F1 Japanese Grand Prix.

image.altText: Wrecked and mangled F1 car on the back of the truck

items.title: Labor wins blue-ribbon seat of Morphett from Liberals

children.content: Liberal frontbencher Stephen Patterson loses his bid to be re-elected in the beachside seat of Morphett in the South Australian state election.

gallery.altText: A bald man with glasses wearing a suit jacket and a purple shirt

thumbnail.altText: A bald man with glasses wearing a suit jacket and a purple shirt

items.title: Dragons playmaker Flanagan hospitalised following head knock against Gold Coast

children.content: St George Illawarra five-eighth Kyle Flanagan was transported to hospital after a head knock in the Dragons' 22-14 loss to the Gold Coast Titans.

image.altText: Kyle Flanagan is stretchered off the field during the Dragons loss to Gold Coast

items.title: Demons overwhelm Blues in late MCG surge

children.content: The Demons complete an amazing comeback and pile pressure on Carlton coach Michael Voss, while West Coast celebrates an inspirational victory on the road against Port Adelaide.

image.altText: An excited Melbourne player roars in celebration after kicking a goal, as he is grabbed by a teammate.

items.title: The best and worst things about being on reality TV, according to Guy Sebastian

children.content: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

gallery.altText: Guy Sebastian sits behind a table smiling. Behind him are shop mannequins and sale signs

thumbnail.altText: Guy Sebastian sits behind a table smiling. Behind him are shop mannequins and sale signs

items.title: Quiz: Can you go back in time and nail our quiz?

children.content: Cast your mind back and see if you can conquer this week's round of trivia.

image.altText: Phar Lap

items.title: You, Me & Tuscany marks the return of the romcom — and the audience is aboard

children.content: You, Me & Tuscany is highly anticipated by fans of Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page, who can't wait for a rare romantic comedy to hit cinemas.

image.altText: Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page in character looking into each other's eyes outside in a field

items.title: No transport relief for Sydneysiders as fuel prices surge

children.content: The NSW Liberals and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union (RTBU) have renewed calls for free public transport in response to surging fuel prices.

articles.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April as fuel prices climb

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

image.altText: A train stops at a station while commuters wait on the platform alongside it.

articles.title: Free public transport in Tasmania for three months to counter fuel crisis

children.content: The Tasmanian government says public transport will be free statewide for three months, starting on Monday, in response to surging fuel prices.

image.altText: Metro bus

gallery.altText: train commuters walk through the opal ticket machines at sydney's opal railway station

thumbnail.altText: train commuters walk through the opal ticket machines at sydney's opal railway station

items.title: How Australia is supporting NASA's first Moon flight in 50 years

children.content: Canberra-based communications systems will support NASA's first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century, helping astronauts orbit the Moon and return data.

image.altText: Three people look at space communications technology.

items.title: 'It's a nightmare': Complaints pile up over Booking.com grievances

children.content: Hundreds of complaints against Booking.com are piling up as customers and hosts allege they have lost money, their property has been trashed and no-one is taking responsibility.

image.altText: A laptop on a table with the booking.com website, a coconut drink and two logos of a palm and umbrella.

items.title: Annie is only 26, but will die within months. Here's what she wants you to know

children.content: Before ending her life through voluntary assisted dying, Annaliese Holland shares her final wishes and advice on living a full life.

gallery.altText: A young woman with long blonde hair wears a puffer jacket while sitting on a park bench, looking at the camera with a smile.

items.title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump draw crowds across US and Europe

children.content: Huge crowds rally against US President Donald Trump from coast to coast in what organisers hope will be the largest single-day protest in US history.

image.altText: A woman dressed as the Statue of Liberty taking part in the protest.

items.title: 'A lover of life': Mal Leyland eyes off one final trip before dementia decline

children.content: Adventurer and filmmaker Mal Leyland, of Ask the Leyland Brothers fame, shares his private health battle. But the TV legend is determined to retrace the cross-country trip that made him famous 60 years ago.

image.altText: A man in his early 80s wearing a collared shirt has his arm around his adult daughter, she wears black singlet and short hair

items.title: Charlie was at the airport to pick me up. We've been 'us' ever since

children.content: After cancelling a full Sri Lankan wedding and a string of "disastrous" relationships, Sashi Perera found herself opposite a man who made believing in him simple.

gallery.altText: Comedian Sashi Perera smiles with her husband on a London street, wearing a patterned red skirt and black top.

thumbnail.altText: Comedian Sashi Perera smiles with her husband on a London street, wearing a patterned red skirt and black top.

avatar.alt: Portrait of writer and comedian Sashi Perera wearing a long shirt dress that is pale purple, with a green skirt.

items.title: Why the 'perfect pair' of jeans remains an elusive concept

children.content: The enduring challenge of finding the "perfect pair" of jeans highlights the garment's cultural significance, and the opportunity to move toward more sustainable and more thoughtfully designed denim.

image.altText: A rack of denim jeans of various shades of blue on coathangers

items.title: Hastie warns Australians may be losing faith in US alliance

children.content: Coalition frontbencher Andrew Hastie has warned the credibility of the United States is being damaged and says Australians may be losing faith in the alliance.

image.altText: A man in a suit speaks to another man in a suit on a tv studio set.

items.title: Confronting images of 'unprecedented' outback flood after cyclone

children.content: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

image.altText: What appears to be a goat seeks shelter in a car while being pummelled with fast moving flood water.

articles.title: Emergency relief announced as cleanup begins for ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Structures have been destroyed and more than 1000 people remain without power as the cleanup begins in Western Australia following ex-tropical cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: A worker is repairing electricity transmission lines.

gallery.altText: A man paddleboards through a rural property

thumbnail.altText: A man paddleboards through a rural property

items.title: Kings one win away from NBL title after beating 36ers

children.content: The Sydney Kings edge a step closer to winning a sixth NBL championship following a 106-93 defeat of the Adelaide 36ers in front of a record crowd.

image.altText: Kendric Davis shoots during the NBL championship series between the Sydney Kings and Adelaide 36ers.

items.title: Roos star sent to AFL tribunal after alleged blood-wiping incident

children.content: North Melbourne ruckman Tristan Xerri is sent directly to the AFL tribunal on a charge of serious misconduct after he allegedly wiped blood on Essendon captain Andy McGrath's face.

image.altText: Tristan Xerri of North Melbourne (centre) and Andrew McGrath of Essendon (second from right) get involved in an altercation.

items.title: Vital Aboriginal records saved in the 70s by an astute WA archivist

children.content: Margaret Medcalf, who turns 100 this week, insisted that decades of Aboriginal welfare records be preserved in the state archives and not destroyed. Those documents have proved essential to connecting Aboriginal people with their family history.

image.altText: black and white photo of Margaret Medcalf in the archives in 1969

items.title: Mass casualty exercise in Adelaide draws details from recent events

children.content: South Australian emergency services conduct their largest mass casuality exercise in history in a bid to test coordination between agencies.

image.altText: A unit with smoke coming from it on a footpath with people lying nearby.

items.title: The extraordinary story of Australia's oldest living Olympian and his remarkable debut

children.content: Gordon "Wingnut" Ingate was twice denied time off work to compete in the Olympic Games in his 20s, but continued to excel as a sailor, eventually getting the chance to compete in his 40s at Munich 1972.

items.title: The unexpected type of man more women want to date

children.content: For the first time, more men than women are "marrying up" the education ladder, toppling a millennia-old tradition, an exclusive ABC analysis has revealed.

items.title: Carroll College to overturn 32-year ban on students walking to school

children.content: A misunderstanding between the Catholic secondary school and the local council led to the three-decade ban.

image.altText: Students walking in front of a sign

items.title: Family's living experiment reveals Australia's abysmal apartment design

children.content: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family decide to rent an apartment as a trial. Will high-rise living be a realistic alternative to a house as a place to bring up our child?

gallery.altText: Inside the Southbank, Victoria, home of the McMurtie family.
